creator: j@uriah.heep.sax.de
text:
As mburgett@awen.com wrote:

> ... Shouldn't mount_nfs be
> returning an error if the requested mount is aready in place (like
> mounting local filesystems do) and refuse to create the second entry in
> the table?

No, all 4.4BSD mounts can be stacked on the same mountpoint.  UFS
mounts only don't succeed in this since the resource (partition) is
busy, and cannot be mounted again anywhere else.  This is not the case
for MFS mounts, or mounts of other (non-device) resources.

mount -a should, however, be modified to not blindly mount something
that's already mounted.  This is indeed a bug.  I'm not sure whether
there's already an existing PR for it, but i think so.  You apparently
didn't bother to check the open PRs either, did you? :-)

> Conversely, umount -ft nfs won't succeed if the network is down ( error
> returned is 'nfs send error 50' ) if the network is down.

There's an open PR for it.  Our NFS maintainer is, unfortunately, out
to lunch.

-- 
cheers, J"org

joerg_wunsch@uriah.heep.sax.de -- http://www.sax.de/~joerg/ -- NIC: JW11-RIPE
Never trust an operating system you don't have sources for. ;-)
